data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: Instagram utilizes a microservices architecture to handle its massive user base and functionality demands.
data.technical_spec: Deployment of services in containers, often using Docker and orchestrated by Kubernetes.

data.title: Cloud Infrastructure
data.description: Utilizes cloud services to ensure scalability, reliability, and global reach.
data.technical_spec: Integration with AWS or other cloud providers for resources like storage (S3), computing (EC2), and CDN (CloudFront).

data.title: Graph Databases
data.description: Employs graph databases to efficiently manage user relationships and content feed.
data.technical_spec: Neo4j or similar systems for relationships and Neo4j-Cypher for query language.

data.title: Content Delivery Network (CDN)
data.description: Leverages CDN for fast content delivery across the globe.
data.technical_spec: Use of Edge servers and caching techniques to reduce latency, likely Akamai or CloudFront.

data.title: RESTful and GraphQL APIs
data.description: Provides APIs for programmatic access to data and services.
data.technical_spec: Implementation using REST and GraphQL for flexible data queries. Utilizes OAuth2 for authentication.
